sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 13.57 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.13)

statement.latex: Let$$be an automorphism of prime order$p$of a finite group$G$such that$C_G( ) Z(G)$. [r]{a)} Is$G$soluble if$p=3$? V.\,D.\,Mazurov and T.\,L.\,Nedo\-re\-zov proved in (Algebra and Logic, { 35}, no.\,6 (1996), 392--397) that the group$G$is soluble for$p=2$, and there are examples of unsoluble$G$for all$p>3$. [r]{b)} If$G$is soluble, is the derived length of$G$bounded in terms of$p$? [r]{c)} (V.\,K....
